Specimen information

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
Renal function is mildly impaired (elevated serum creatinine). Urinalysis shows significant proteinuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), suggesting possible renal pathology or urinary tract disorder. No evidence of infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). Blood glucose is normal, but HbA1c is unexpectedly low—may indicate analytical error or hemoglobinopathy. Recommend clinical correlation and further nephrological evaluation.
